reviews.review_msg: His lecture slides were very organized and well-made. His lectures were easy to understand, and he didnt just read off the slides, he actually explained the content beyond what was in the slides, which is why going to lectures is very helpful. Exams were fairly made, but the grading is very very tough, so you gotta really know ur stuff
its like they fail you purposely so you're forced to retake it so the school gets more money from you

I LOVED this course at the start with Nadine but when Claydon took over, I have grown to hate this course. The way Claydon and the TA, Lara, mark is ridiculous. They do not encourage students to learn and search for ways for students to fail. Its like they have fun finding ways to not give students full marks for nearly perfect answers.
doesn't care about students and horrible at explaining topics

I loved the beginning of this course despite how difficult it was but I have come to hate it due to Claydon switching in as our professor. His lectures are so bland and his tests are made to trick students. I went to talk to him about a question I felt was unfair and he just smirked and was happy he tricked students...  #Bring Nadine back

The first half of this class I felt so much more engaged with the content (shoutout to Nadine), however I cannot say the same for the second half of the course with Claydon. I felt like the marking was unfair and there were questions to trick you despite him saying he wouldn't do that. His teaching is also dry and he doesn't answer questions well.

Dr Claydon was one of the worst professors I have had. His lectures are bland and he bascially just reads off the slides. His tests are unfair with questions to try and trick students and when I talked to him about how one of the questions was unfair he basically laughed in my face. Doesn't care about his students at all

I found that he sometimes did not know some of his slides properly which made it really hard to understand what he was saying. On top of that the exams were super complex because he wanted you to be super detailed on 50 min midterm exams.

Expects you to regurgitate the entire course onto the midterm in large essay style paragraphs. 45% class average on MT2 and he told the class he was disappointed and we should just try harder lol. Said that we needed to be able to describe the material at length but his lectures are power point diagrams with sparse notes. The dichotomy!

Thomas Claydon doesn't care about his students at all.  You need to remember every word on every lecture slide or you won't do well on the exams. The second midterm had a failing grade and he blamed the class for the poor grade instead of reflecting on his poor teaching ability and unfair exams. would highly recommend choosing another professor.
